title: Evidence-pack drift — auditor-facing vs pre-production evidence
exclusions: Does NOT evaluate the content of the evidence itself; only the SHA256 of the compiled pack.
evidence: Saved search 'evidence_pack_drift' (after each publish) checks the published pack SHA256 against the pre-production manifest.

compliance.assurance_rationale: CC1.5 accountability for controls includes evidence integrity.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that SOC-2 CC1.5 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.47.4: Evidence-pack drift — auditor-facing vs pre-production evidence.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.47.4' running on Evidence-pack build pipeline, publication manifest, auditor-delivery signature events., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that SOX-ITGC ITGC.Evidence.Integrity is enforced — Splunk UC-22.47.4: Evidence-pack drift — auditor-facing vs pre-production evidence.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A pack published to the auditor has a different SHA256 than the pre-production manifest; the UC fires and names the pack.

dataSources: Evidence-pack build pipeline, publication manifest, auditor-delivery signature events.
spl:
index=grc sourcetype=evidence_pack:publish earliest=-30d
| stats latest(sha256) as published_sha latest(pack_id) as pack by _time
| join pack [search index=grc sourcetype=evidence_pack:preprod | stats latest(sha256) as preprod_sha by pack]
| where published_sha!=preprod_sha
| table pack published_sha preprod_sha
description: Compares the pack published to an auditor against the pack that passed internal QA. Divergence is either a reproducibility failure or a tamper event.
value: Protects the integrity of the evidence that underpins the external audit.
implementation: (1) Sign both preprod and published packs; (2) run post-publish; (3) on mismatch, alert internal audit and QA pipeline. Indexes required: `index=grc`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=evidence_pack:preprod`, `sourcetype=evidence_pack:publish`.
visualization: Table of mismatches, single-value 'packs with matching SHA (30d)'.
knownFalsePositives: Evidence stale dates during quarterly cycles, control owner transitions, or platform migrations.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Evidence-pack build pipeline, publication manifest, auditor-delivery signature events..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Sign both preprod and published packs; (2) run post-publish; (3) on mismatch, alert internal audit and QA pipeline.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=grc sourcetype=evidence_pack:publish earliest=-30d
| stats latest(sha256) as published_sha latest(pack_id) as pack by _time
| join pack [search index=grc sourcetype=evidence_pack:preprod | stats latest(sha256) as preprod_sha by pack]
| where published_sha!=preprod_sha
| table pack published_sha preprod_sha
```

Understanding this SPL

**Evidence-pack drift — auditor-facing vs pre-production evidence** — Protects the integrity of the evidence that underpins the external audit.

Documented **Data sources**: Evidence-pack build pipeline, publication manifest, auditor-delivery signature events. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: grc; **sourcetype**: evidence_pack:publish. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=grc, sourcetype=evidence_pack:publish, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` aggregates events by **_time**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where published_sha!=preprod_sha` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: _time, pack, pack], preprod_sha, published_sha.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the GRC system and the control testing or evidence calendar so stale dates are not confused with real control failure.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of mismatches, single-value 'packs with matching SHA (30d)'.
grandmaExplanation: We help you see when control evidence is getting old so you refresh proof before the next review.
